duration: first bird for about 5 minutes, second for about 1-2 minutes
description: Larger than BASA, upright posture, bright ochre-buff on chest, upper belly fading posteriorly. Plain face, yellow legs. scaly back, dark crown, spotting on sides of chest. See photo. Two birds present on mudflats, about 100 yards apart. Birds looked pretty much identical as far as I could tellBehaviors: One was standing on sandy portion of flats near Killdeer, standing upright, walking, bending over to pick here and there at sandflat surface. The other was high up on mudflat with a number of various peeps. Feeding style same.Call: note heardPlumage: juvenile
